Untitled Project
GHOST Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Requirements
Traceability
  • VIEWS
  • Document
  • Table
  • Traceability
  • Deep Traceability

    • 1. 1. Purpose and Scope
    This document defines the GHOST runtime orchestration requirements for the Prompt Builder path.

    The runtime pipeline is organized into four high-level functional stages:
    1. Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions, including normalization, routing, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.
    2. Knowledge Management Path.
    3. Memory Management Path.
    4. Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition.
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE
      2.8.1. Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor UID: GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-FUT-ROOT GHOST Future Vision and Lifecycle Synchronization Scope
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      • GHOST-AGENT-STACK-ROOT Agent Stack Scope
      • GHOST-PREPROCESS-ROOT Preprocessing and Prompt Functions Scope
      • GHOST-KM-ROOT Knowledge Management Scope
      • GHOST-DOC-INGEST-ROOT Document Ingestion Scope
      • GHOST-DOC-RETRIEVE-ROOT Document Retrieval and Reranking Scope
      • GHOST-EVIDENCE-ROOT Evidence Selection and Condensation Scope
      • GHOST-MEMORY-ROOT Memory Management Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-REC-ROOT Memory Recording and Files Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-IR-ROOT Memory Ingestion and Retrieval Scope
      • GHOST-MEM-MERGE-ROOT Memory Merge, ActiveBrief, and Blackboard Boundary Scope
      • GHOST-CONTEXT-INTEGRATION-ROOT Context Integration and Prompt Assembly Scope
      • GHOST-UI-ROOT GHOST Interface Family Scope
      • GHOST-RUN-OPS-CAPABILITY AWS Runtime Platform and Operations Capability
      • GHOST-QGO-ROOT GHOST Quality, Governance, and Observability Scope
      STATEMENT: This requirement is the parent anchor for the approved StrictDoc child-file tree. Connected child dummy files shall reference this UID, or a child-file parent UID, through StrictDoc parent references so generated HTML can display navigable requirement relationships.
    GHOST-ORCH-ROOT
    1.1. Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-ORCH-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
    • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State
    • GHOST-ORCH-CONTROLLER-ROLE Controller Coordination Role
    • GHOST-ORCH-LOWER-LEVEL-OWNERSHIP Lower-Level Subsystem Ownership
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
    • GHOST-ORCH-DATA-ROOTS Controller Data Root Contract
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-AGENTSTACK Agent Stack Boundary
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-DOCUMENT-INGESTION Document Ingestion Boundary
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-DOCUMENT-RETRIEVAL Document Retrieval Boundary
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-MEMORY Memory Management Boundary
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-GUI GUI Boundary
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-QUALITY Quality and Observability Boundary
    STATEMENT: This file shall define the runtime orchestration and pipeline coordination requirements for GHOST Prompt Builder execution.

    The scope includes prompt preprocessing, prompt shaping, document/project knowledge retrieval, memory retrieval, document evidence processing, memory context synthesis, shared SuperPrompt state, and final prompt composition.

    The scope excludes detailed requirements for document ingestion, memory recording, memory ingestion, GUI layout, deployment, benchmarking, logging sinks, and future lifecycle synchronization except where this file must reference their runtime boundaries.
    • GHOST-ORCH-PRODUCT-NAME
      1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-PRODUCT-NAME STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: The product described by this document shall be named GHOST.

      The existing Python package namespace may remain ragstream in current source code and paths.

      Requirements shall not treat ragstream as the product identity.
    • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE
      2.1. SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State UID: GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-SNAPSHOT-CONTRACT Runtime Snapshot Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-SAME-SP Same SuperPrompt Instance Across Runtime
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-SUPERPROMPT AC: SuperPrompt Context Separation
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall use SuperPrompt as the shared state object passed through Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions, context retrieval, document evidence processing, memory context synthesis, and final prompt composition.

      SuperPrompt shall carry canonical prompt body fields, runtime extras, effective retrieval query text, document context artifacts, memory context artifacts, stage history, and rendered prompt strings.
    • GHOST-ORCH-CONTROLLER-ROLE
      2.2. Controller Coordination Role UID: GHOST-ORCH-CONTROLLER-ROLE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: The application controller shall coordinate runtime calls across preprocessing, PromptShaper, document retriever, memory retriever, document reranker, A3, A4, and final prompt projection.

      The controller shall not own the internal algorithms of document retrieval, memory retrieval, reranking, A3 classification, A4 condensation, or memory synthesis.
    • GHOST-ORCH-LOWER-LEVEL-OWNERSHIP
      2.3. Lower-Level Subsystem Ownership UID: GHOST-ORCH-LOWER-LEVEL-OWNERSHIP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: This file shall define orchestration-level runtime responsibilities and boundaries.

      Detailed algorithms shall remain owned by their subsystem requirement files:
      - preprocessing, ActiveBrief relation classification, PromptShaper, and related prompt-function behavior;
      - neutral agent-stack behavior;
      - document retrieval, dense/SPLADE scoring, RRF, reranking, A3, and A4;
      - memory recording, indexing, retrieval, compression, and synthesis;
      - SuperPrompt projection and rendering.
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY
      3.1. Four Functional Runtime Stages UID: GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY Stage 3: Memory Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-PREPROCESS-A2 Preprocessing to PromptShaper Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-CONTEXT-PATHS Context Path Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-RETRIEVAL-BOUNDARY Retrieval Boundary Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-FOUR-STAGES AC: Four Main Functional Stages
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-KNOWLEDGE-MEMORY-PEER AC: Knowledge and Memory Peer Paths
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-CONDENSATION-PLACEMENT AC: Context Processing Placement
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall be described by these four high-level functional stages:

      1. Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions.
      2. Knowledge Management Path.
      3. Memory Management Path.
      4. Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition.

      Prompt normalization, ActiveBrief relation routing, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring shall belong to Stage 1 and shall not be represented as separate high-level functional stages.

      Each stage shall have clear inputs, responsibilities, outputs, and ownership boundaries.
    • GHOST-ORCH-DATA-ROOTS
      9.1. Controller Data Root Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-DATA-ROOTS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PROJECT-SELECTION Active Project Selection Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-CONFIG Memory Retrieval Configuration Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-RUNTIME-FLAGS Runtime Feature Flags
      • GHOST-ORCH-TOP-K Retrieval Top-K Runtime Setting
      STATEMENT: The controller shall maintain runtime paths for project document raw data, document Chroma stores, document SPLADE stores, memory root, and memory SQLite index.

      The controller shall ensure required runtime root directories exist before they are used.
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-AGENTSTACK
      10.1. Agent Stack Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-AGENTSTACK STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed PromptShaper selector catalogs, agent prompt definitions, model-call behavior, parsing behavior, and agent configuration shall be owned by the Agent Stack requirement file.

      This file shall reference PromptShaper only at runtime orchestration level.
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-DOCUMENT-INGESTION
      10.2. Document Ingestion Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-DOCUMENT-INGESTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed requirements for creating document stores, chunking source files, embedding documents, SPLADE indexing, and ingestion status shall be owned by Document Ingestion and Knowledge Management requirement files.

      This file shall reference document stores only as runtime inputs to the Knowledge Management path.
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-DOCUMENT-RETRIEVAL
      10.3. Document Retrieval Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-DOCUMENT-RETRIEVAL STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed requirements for dense retrieval scoring, SPLADE scoring, RRF settings, hydration edge cases, reranker model configuration, A3 logic, and A4 condenser prompt behavior shall be owned by Knowledge Management and RAG pipeline requirement files.

      This file shall state how those capabilities fit into the Knowledge Management path.
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-MEMORY
      10.4. Memory Management Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-MEMORY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed requirements for memory recording, memory ingestion, memory retrieval, memory scoring, memory compression, ActiveBrief generation, memory file persistence, and memory metadata/index handling shall be owned by Memory Management requirement files.

      This file shall state how those capabilities fit into the Memory Management path.
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-GUI
      10.5. GUI Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-GUI STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed requirements for Streamlit layout, widgets, visual indicators, manual buttons, and workspace layout shall be owned by GUI requirement files.

      This file shall define only the runtime orchestration contract behind Prompt Builder execution.
    • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-QUALITY
      10.6. Quality and Observability Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-QUALITY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      STATEMENT: Detailed requirements for logging, metrics, benchmarking, security, traceability, and verification governance shall be owned by Quality, Governance, and Observability requirement files.

      This file shall include only acceptance criteria needed to validate the orchestrator runtime hierarchy.
    • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT
      1.1. Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State
      • GHOST-ORCH-CONTROLLER-ROLE Controller Coordination Role
      • GHOST-ORCH-LOWER-LEVEL-OWNERSHIP Lower-Level Subsystem Ownership

      STATEMENT: This file shall define the runtime orchestration and pipeline coordination requirements for GHOST Prompt Builder execution.

      The scope includes prompt preprocessing, prompt shaping, document/project knowledge retrieval, memory retrieval, document evidence processing, memory context synthesis, shared SuperPrompt state, and final prompt composition.

      The scope excludes detailed requirements for document ingestion, memory recording, memory ingestion, GUI layout, deployment, benchmarking, logging sinks, and future lifecycle synchronization except where this file must reference their runtime boundaries.
    GHOST-ORCH-PRODUCT-NAME
    1.2. Product and Package Naming Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-PRODUCT-NAME STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
    STATEMENT: The product described by this document shall be named GHOST.

    The existing Python package namespace may remain ragstream in current source code and paths.

    Requirements shall not treat ragstream as the product identity.

    2. 2. Runtime Object Model and Boundaries
    GHOST runtime orchestration uses a shared SuperPrompt object as the evolving in-memory state for the current Prompt Builder run.

    The controller coordinates executable components. The lower-level subsystems own their internal algorithms and data stores.
    • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT
      1.1. Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State

      • GHOST-ORCH-LOWER-LEVEL-OWNERSHIP Lower-Level Subsystem Ownership

      STATEMENT: This file shall define the runtime orchestration and pipeline coordination requirements for GHOST Prompt Builder execution.

      The scope includes prompt preprocessing, prompt shaping, document/project knowledge retrieval, memory retrieval, document evidence processing, memory context synthesis, shared SuperPrompt state, and final prompt composition.

      The scope excludes detailed requirements for document ingestion, memory recording, memory ingestion, GUI layout, deployment, benchmarking, logging sinks, and future lifecycle synchronization except where this file must reference their runtime boundaries.
    GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE
    2.1. SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State UID: GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-ORCH-SNAPSHOT-CONTRACT Runtime Snapshot Contract
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-SAME-SP Same SuperPrompt Instance Across Runtime
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-SUPERPROMPT AC: SuperPrompt Context Separation
    STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall use SuperPrompt as the shared state object passed through Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions, context retrieval, document evidence processing, memory context synthesis, and final prompt composition.

    SuperPrompt shall carry canonical prompt body fields, runtime extras, effective retrieval query text, document context artifacts, memory context artifacts, stage history, and rendered prompt strings.
    • GHOST-ORCH-SNAPSHOT-CONTRACT
      2.4. Runtime Snapshot Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-SNAPSHOT-CONTRACT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall preserve stage snapshots of the evolving SuperPrompt where the GUI or manual workflow requires inspection of intermediate state.

      Snapshots shall represent runtime state at meaningful orchestration points and shall not redefine the functional pipeline hierarchy.
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-SAME-SP
      8.1. Same SuperPrompt Instance Across Runtime UID: GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-SAME-SP STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State
      STATEMENT: The runtime shall pass the same evolving SuperPrompt object through the main Prompt Builder path unless a snapshot copy is intentionally created for inspection.

      Subsystems shall mutate and return the current SuperPrompt according to their ownership boundary.
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-SUPERPROMPT
      11.4. AC: SuperPrompt Context Separation UID: GHOST-ORCH-AC-SUPERPROMPT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State
      STATEMENT: The final SuperPrompt state shall be able to contain structured prompt data, document evidence artifacts, memory context artifacts, and rendered prompt-ready output without collapsing them into one indistinguishable context source.

    GHOST-ORCH-SNAPSHOT-CONTRACT
    2.4. Runtime Snapshot Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-SNAPSHOT-CONTRACT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State
    STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall preserve stage snapshots of the evolving SuperPrompt where the GUI or manual workflow requires inspection of intermediate state.

    Snapshots shall represent runtime state at meaningful orchestration points and shall not redefine the functional pipeline hierarchy.

    3. 3. Four Functional Runtime Stages
    The Prompt Builder pipeline is defined at high level by four functional stages. A0 preprocessing, routing, and A2 PromptShaper / Job Structuring are internal functions of the first stage, even when they execute through separate Python methods, buttons, or snapshots.
    • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT
      1.1. Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-ROOT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-SRS-FILE-TREE Requirement File Tree Parent Anchor
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PRODUCT-NAME Product and Package Naming Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-SUPERPROMPT-CENTRAL-STATE SuperPrompt as Shared Runtime State
      • GHOST-ORCH-CONTROLLER-ROLE Controller Coordination Role
      • GHOST-ORCH-LOWER-LEVEL-OWNERSHIP Lower-Level Subsystem Ownership
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      • GHOST-ORCH-DATA-ROOTS Controller Data Root Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-AGENTSTACK Agent Stack Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-DOCUMENT-INGESTION Document Ingestion Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-DOCUMENT-RETRIEVAL Document Retrieval Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-MEMORY Memory Management Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-GUI GUI Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-BOUNDARY-QUALITY Quality and Observability Boundary
      STATEMENT: This file shall define the runtime orchestration and pipeline coordination requirements for GHOST Prompt Builder execution.

      The scope includes prompt preprocessing, prompt shaping, document/project knowledge retrieval, memory retrieval, document evidence processing, memory context synthesis, shared SuperPrompt state, and final prompt composition.

      The scope excludes detailed requirements for document ingestion, memory recording, memory ingestion, GUI layout, deployment, benchmarking, logging sinks, and future lifecycle synchronization except where this file must reference their runtime boundaries.
    GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY
    3.1. Four Functional Runtime Stages UID: GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY Stage 3: Memory Management Path
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-PREPROCESS-A2 Preprocessing to PromptShaper Coordination
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-CONTEXT-PATHS Context Path Coordination
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-RETRIEVAL-BOUNDARY Retrieval Boundary Coordination
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-FOUR-STAGES AC: Four Main Functional Stages
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-KNOWLEDGE-MEMORY-PEER AC: Knowledge and Memory Peer Paths
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-CONDENSATION-PLACEMENT AC: Context Processing Placement
    STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall be described by these four high-level functional stages:

    1. Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions.
    2. Knowledge Management Path.
    3. Memory Management Path.
    4. Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition.

    Prompt normalization, ActiveBrief relation routing, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring shall belong to Stage 1 and shall not be represented as separate high-level functional stages.

    Each stage shall have clear inputs, responsibilities, outputs, and ownership boundaries.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE
      3.3. Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: Stage 2 shall produce document/project knowledge context for the current request.

      It shall include document retrieval, document ranking/reranking behavior, document usefulness validation, and document evidence condensation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY
      3.4. Stage 3: Memory Management Path UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-SCOPE Memory Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: Stage 3 shall produce durable-memory-based context for the current request.

      It shall include memory record lookup, memory vector retrieval, memory candidate scoring and selection, memory compression, ActiveBrief use, and synthesized memory context generation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION
      3.5. Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-SCOPE Final Composition Scope
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-COMPOSITION-LAST Composition After Context Availability
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-COMPOSITION AC: Final Composition Inputs
      STATEMENT: Stage 4 shall integrate the structured prompt, selected document evidence, synthesized memory context, and applicable system/configuration rules into the final Prompt Builder output.

      The composed output shall be the Prompt Builder artifact presented to the user.
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-PREPROCESS-A2
      8.2. Preprocessing to PromptShaper Coordination UID: GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-PREPROCESS-A2 STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      STATEMENT: Within Stage 1, the controller shall run preprocessing and routing before PromptShaper.

      PromptShaper shall receive a SuperPrompt that already contains normalized body content and routing metadata.
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-CONTEXT-PATHS
      8.3. Context Path Coordination UID: GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-CONTEXT-PATHS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      STATEMENT: The runtime shall coordinate Knowledge Management and Memory Management as separate context paths that both read the current SuperPrompt and write their own context artifacts back into it.

      Document context artifacts and memory context artifacts shall not overwrite each other.
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-RETRIEVAL-BOUNDARY
      8.4. Retrieval Boundary Coordination UID: GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-RETRIEVAL-BOUNDARY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      STATEMENT: The controller retrieval boundary shall call the document Retriever and, when configured, the MemoryRetriever.

      Document retrieval shall write document chunk artifacts.

      Memory retrieval shall write memory pack and synthesized memory context artifacts.
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-FOUR-STAGES
      11.1. AC: Four Main Functional Stages UID: GHOST-ORCH-AC-FOUR-STAGES STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall preserve the following four functional orchestration stages as the main runtime model:

      1. Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions.
      2. Knowledge Management Path.
      3. Memory Management Path.
      4. Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition.

      A0 preprocessing, routing, and A2 PromptShaper / Job Structuring may remain separate executable methods, runner steps, GUI indicators, and snapshots, but they shall remain traceable to the single Stage 1 capability rather than being represented as separate high-level functional stages.
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-KNOWLEDGE-MEMORY-PEER
      11.2. AC: Knowledge and Memory Peer Paths UID: GHOST-ORCH-AC-KNOWLEDGE-MEMORY-PEER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall maintain Knowledge Management and Memory Management as separate peer runtime context paths after Stage 1 has completed prompt preprocessing, routing, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Document context artifacts and memory context artifacts shall keep separate ownership, separate processing, and separate SuperPrompt write-back fields.
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-CONDENSATION-PLACEMENT
      11.3. AC: Context Processing Placement UID: GHOST-ORCH-AC-CONDENSATION-PLACEMENT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall place document reranking, document usefulness validation, and document evidence condensation inside the Knowledge Management Path.

      The Prompt Builder runtime shall place memory scoring, memory compression, ActiveBrief use, and MemoryMergeSynthesizer output inside the Memory Management Path.
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY
      3.1. Four Functional Runtime Stages UID: GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY Stage 3: Memory Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-PREPROCESS-A2 Preprocessing to PromptShaper Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-CONTEXT-PATHS Context Path Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-RETRIEVAL-BOUNDARY Retrieval Boundary Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-FOUR-STAGES AC: Four Main Functional Stages
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-KNOWLEDGE-MEMORY-PEER AC: Knowledge and Memory Peer Paths
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-CONDENSATION-PLACEMENT AC: Context Processing Placement
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall be described by these four high-level functional stages:

      1. Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions.
      2. Knowledge Management Path.
      3. Memory Management Path.
      4. Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition.

      Prompt normalization, ActiveBrief relation routing, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring shall belong to Stage 1 and shall not be represented as separate high-level functional stages.

      Each stage shall have clear inputs, responsibilities, outputs, and ownership boundaries.
    GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
    3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
    STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

    Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

    Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT
      4.1. Raw Prompt Input Handling UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall receive the raw user prompt text from the active Prompt Builder input.

      Whitespace-only input shall not create a meaningful SuperPrompt transformation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA
      4.2. Prompt Schema Normalization UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall use the prompt schema to normalize the raw user prompt into SuperPrompt body fields.

      The canonical body shall support fields such as system, task, audience, role, tone, depth, confidence, evidence_policy, context, purpose, format, and text.
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION
      4.3. ActiveBrief Relation Classification UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall classify the relation between the current prompt and the active memory brief when memory context exists.

      The classification shall support materiality and topic-relation routing signals such as strong or weak prompt materiality and same-topic, related-domain, or irrelevant topic relation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING
      4.4. Retrieval Routing Preparation UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall prepare routing information used later to build retrieval intent.

      Routing shall allow current prompt text, ActiveBrief body, ActiveBrief title, or empty document retrieval intent depending on the prompt materiality and topic relation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY
      4.5. Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: After preprocessing, the runtime may compose a prompt-ready preview from the current SuperPrompt state.

      This preview shall not be treated as the final Prompt Builder output when later context paths still have to run.
    • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT
      4.6. Preprocessing Output Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall output a mutated SuperPrompt containing normalized prompt body content and routing metadata in extras.

      The normalized and routed SuperPrompt shall be suitable for the PromptShaper internal function and retrieval-intent construction within Stage 1.
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT
      4.7. PromptShaper Input Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: Within Stage 1, PromptShaper shall receive the current SuperPrompt after preprocessing and routing.

      It shall use the prompt body and runtime metadata as the basis for job structuring.
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE
      4.8. PromptShaper Structured Job Output UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: PromptShaper shall read the current structured prompt from SuperPrompt and select job/configuration metadata for the request.

      The selected metadata shall include supported fields such as system, audience, tone, depth, confidence, and evidence policy where available.

      PromptShaper shall update the same SuperPrompt instance and preserve the task, purpose, and context produced by preprocessing unless a supported selector result explicitly updates a compatible field.
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION
      4.9. PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: PromptShaper shall store selected meta-setting identifiers in SuperPrompt extras when such selections are produced.

      This metadata shall be available to prompt projection, including evidence policy rendering.
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION
      4.10. PromptShaper LLM Execution Option UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: The runtime shall allow PromptShaper execution with or without the LLM-assisted path according to active runtime settings.

      Disabling the LLM-assisted PromptShaper path shall not remove the SuperPrompt state object from the pipeline.
    • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT
      4.11. PromptShaper Output Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      STATEMENT: The PromptShaper internal function shall output the same SuperPrompt instance updated with structured job content and PromptShaper metadata.

      The output shall be suitable for retrieval-intent construction and both context paths.
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY
      3.1. Four Functional Runtime Stages UID: GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY Stage 3: Memory Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-PREPROCESS-A2 Preprocessing to PromptShaper Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-CONTEXT-PATHS Context Path Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-RETRIEVAL-BOUNDARY Retrieval Boundary Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-FOUR-STAGES AC: Four Main Functional Stages
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-KNOWLEDGE-MEMORY-PEER AC: Knowledge and Memory Peer Paths
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-CONDENSATION-PLACEMENT AC: Context Processing Placement
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall be described by these four high-level functional stages:

      1. Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions.
      2. Knowledge Management Path.
      3. Memory Management Path.
      4. Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition.

      Prompt normalization, ActiveBrief relation routing, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring shall belong to Stage 1 and shall not be represented as separate high-level functional stages.

      Each stage shall have clear inputs, responsibilities, outputs, and ownership boundaries.
    GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE
    3.3. Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
    STATEMENT: Stage 2 shall produce document/project knowledge context for the current request.

    It shall include document retrieval, document ranking/reranking behavior, document usefulness validation, and document evidence condensation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE
      5.1. Knowledge Management Runtime Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-INPUTS Knowledge Path Inputs
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-QUERY Document Retrieval Query Construction
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-DENSE Dense Document Retrieval Backend
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SPLADE SPLADE Document Retrieval Backend
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RRF Document Retrieval Fusion
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-HYDRATION Document Chunk Hydration
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SUPERPROMPT-WRITE Knowledge Context SuperPrompt Write-Back
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RERANKER Document ReRanker Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A3 Document Evidence Usefulness Validation
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A4 Document Evidence Condensation
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-OUTPUT Knowledge Path Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-DOCUMENT-POSTPROCESS Document Post-Retrieval Coordination
      STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall produce document/project context from the active project stores.

      It shall include document retrieval, dense and sparse scoring, deterministic fusion, hydration, document reranking or pass-through, document usefulness validation, and document evidence condensation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY
      3.1. Four Functional Runtime Stages UID: GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY Stage 3: Memory Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-PREPROCESS-A2 Preprocessing to PromptShaper Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-CONTEXT-PATHS Context Path Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-RETRIEVAL-BOUNDARY Retrieval Boundary Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-FOUR-STAGES AC: Four Main Functional Stages
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-KNOWLEDGE-MEMORY-PEER AC: Knowledge and Memory Peer Paths
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-CONDENSATION-PLACEMENT AC: Context Processing Placement
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall be described by these four high-level functional stages:

      1. Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions.
      2. Knowledge Management Path.
      3. Memory Management Path.
      4. Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition.

      Prompt normalization, ActiveBrief relation routing, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring shall belong to Stage 1 and shall not be represented as separate high-level functional stages.

      Each stage shall have clear inputs, responsibilities, outputs, and ownership boundaries.
    GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY
    3.4. Stage 3: Memory Management Path UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-SCOPE Memory Management Runtime Scope
    STATEMENT: Stage 3 shall produce durable-memory-based context for the current request.

    It shall include memory record lookup, memory vector retrieval, memory candidate scoring and selection, memory compression, ActiveBrief use, and synthesized memory context generation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-SCOPE
      6.1. Memory Management Runtime Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-SCOPE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY Stage 3: Memory Management Path
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-DURABLE-TRUTH Durable Memory Truth Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-RETRIEVER-ENTRY MemoryRetriever Entry Point
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-QUERY Memory Retrieval Query Construction
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-SEMANTIC Semantic Memory Retrieval
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-INDEX Indexed Memory Candidate Lookup
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-CANDIDATE-PACK MemoryContextPack Assembly
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-COMPRESSION Memory Candidate Compression
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-ACTIVEBRIEF ActiveBrief Runtime Use
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-SYNTHESIS MemoryMergeSynthesizer Runtime Synthesis
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-SUPERPROMPT-WRITE Memory Context SuperPrompt Write-Back
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-EMPTY-PACK Empty Memory Pack Behavior
      • GHOST-ORCH-MEMORY-OUTPUT Memory Path Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-MEMORY-INTERNAL Memory Internal Processing Coordination
      STATEMENT: The Memory Management path shall produce context from durable GHOST memory rather than from project document stores.

      It shall include memory record truth, memory metadata/index lookup, semantic memory retrieval, memory candidate scoring and selection, memory compression, ActiveBrief use, and synthesized memory context generation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY
      3.1. Four Functional Runtime Stages UID: GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-ROOT Orchestrator and Runtime Pipeline Scope
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-3-MEMORY Stage 3: Memory Management Path
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-PREPROCESS-A2 Preprocessing to PromptShaper Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-CONTEXT-PATHS Context Path Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-RETRIEVAL-BOUNDARY Retrieval Boundary Coordination
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-FOUR-STAGES AC: Four Main Functional Stages
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-KNOWLEDGE-MEMORY-PEER AC: Knowledge and Memory Peer Paths
      • GHOST-ORCH-AC-CONDENSATION-PLACEMENT AC: Context Processing Placement
      STATEMENT: The Prompt Builder runtime shall be described by these four high-level functional stages:

      1. Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions.
      2. Knowledge Management Path.
      3. Memory Management Path.
      4. Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition.

      Prompt normalization, ActiveBrief relation routing, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring shall belong to Stage 1 and shall not be represented as separate high-level functional stages.

      Each stage shall have clear inputs, responsibilities, outputs, and ownership boundaries.
    GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION
    3.5. Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-SCOPE Final Composition Scope
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-COMPOSITION-LAST Composition After Context Availability
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-COMPOSITION AC: Final Composition Inputs
    STATEMENT: Stage 4 shall integrate the structured prompt, selected document evidence, synthesized memory context, and applicable system/configuration rules into the final Prompt Builder output.

    The composed output shall be the Prompt Builder artifact presented to the user.
    • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-SCOPE
      7.1. Final Composition Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-SCOPE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-SYSTEM System and Configuration Rendering
      • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-REQUEST Current User Request Rendering
      • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-MEMORY Memory Context Rendering
      • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-DOCUMENT-SUMMARY Document Evidence Summary Rendering
      • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-RAW-DOCUMENT Raw Document Evidence Rendering
      • GHOST-ORCH-COMPOSE-OUTPUT Prompt Ready Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Final prompt composition shall render the Prompt Builder output from the current SuperPrompt state.

      The output shall include system/configuration content, current user request content, supporting memory context, supporting document evidence summary, and raw document evidence where appropriate for the current stage.
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-COMPOSITION-LAST
      8.7. Composition After Context Availability UID: GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-COMPOSITION-LAST STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
      STATEMENT: Final prompt composition shall occur after the structured prompt and available runtime context artifacts have been written into SuperPrompt.

      Composition shall use whatever document evidence and memory context are actually available and shall not fabricate missing context.
    • GHOST-ORCH-AC-COMPOSITION
      11.5. AC: Final Composition Inputs UID: GHOST-ORCH-AC-COMPOSITION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-4-COMPOSITION Stage 4: Final Context Integration and Prompt Composition
      STATEMENT: The final Prompt Builder output shall be composed from structured prompt content, available document evidence, available synthesized memory context, and system/configuration rules.

      Missing document context or missing memory context shall not be fabricated during final prompt composition.

    4. 4. Stage 1 - Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    This stage owns the complete prompt-preparation capability: raw-prompt normalization, routing, ActiveBrief relation classification, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring. These remain distinguishable internal operations, but they form one high-level functional stage.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT
    4.1. Raw Prompt Input Handling UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall receive the raw user prompt text from the active Prompt Builder input.

    Whitespace-only input shall not create a meaningful SuperPrompt transformation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA
    4.2. Prompt Schema Normalization UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall use the prompt schema to normalize the raw user prompt into SuperPrompt body fields.

    The canonical body shall support fields such as system, task, audience, role, tone, depth, confidence, evidence_policy, context, purpose, format, and text.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION
    4.3. ActiveBrief Relation Classification UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall classify the relation between the current prompt and the active memory brief when memory context exists.

    The classification shall support materiality and topic-relation routing signals such as strong or weak prompt materiality and same-topic, related-domain, or irrelevant topic relation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING
    4.4. Retrieval Routing Preparation UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall prepare routing information used later to build retrieval intent.

    Routing shall allow current prompt text, ActiveBrief body, ActiveBrief title, or empty document retrieval intent depending on the prompt materiality and topic relation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY
    4.5. Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: After preprocessing, the runtime may compose a prompt-ready preview from the current SuperPrompt state.

    This preview shall not be treated as the final Prompt Builder output when later context paths still have to run.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT
    4.6. Preprocessing Output Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: The preprocessing stage shall output a mutated SuperPrompt containing normalized prompt body content and routing metadata in extras.

    The normalized and routed SuperPrompt shall be suitable for the PromptShaper internal function and retrieval-intent construction within Stage 1.
    PromptShaper / Job Structuring is an internal function within Stage 1. It runs after normalization and routing and enriches the same SuperPrompt with explicit job/configuration metadata for downstream context processing and composition.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT
    4.7. PromptShaper Input Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: Within Stage 1, PromptShaper shall receive the current SuperPrompt after preprocessing and routing.

    It shall use the prompt body and runtime metadata as the basis for job structuring.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option

      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE
    4.8. PromptShaper Structured Job Output UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: PromptShaper shall read the current structured prompt from SuperPrompt and select job/configuration metadata for the request.

    The selected metadata shall include supported fields such as system, audience, tone, depth, confidence, and evidence policy where available.

    PromptShaper shall update the same SuperPrompt instance and preserve the task, purpose, and context produced by preprocessing unless a supported selector result explicitly updates a compatible field.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-INPUT PromptShaper Input Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-STRUCTURE PromptShaper Structured Job Output
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION PromptShaper LLM Execution Option
      • GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT PromptShaper Output Contract
      STATEMENT: Stage 1 shall transform the raw user prompt into normalized runtime input, routing information, and an internal structured job representation.

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION
    4.9. PromptShaper Catalog Selection Metadata UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-CATALOG-SELECTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: PromptShaper shall store selected meta-setting identifiers in SuperPrompt extras when such selections are produced.

    This metadata shall be available to prompt projection, including evidence policy rendering.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION
    4.10. PromptShaper LLM Execution Option UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-LLM-OPTION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: The runtime shall allow PromptShaper execution with or without the LLM-assisted path according to active runtime settings.

    Disabling the LLM-assisted PromptShaper path shall not remove the SuperPrompt state object from the pipeline.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS
      3.2. Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-INPUT Raw Prompt Input Handling
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-SCHEMA Prompt Schema Normalization
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-RELATION-CLASSIFICATION ActiveBrief Relation Classification
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-QUERY-ROUTING Retrieval Routing Preparation
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-PROMPT-READY Early Prompt Projection After Preprocessing
      • GHOST-ORCH-PREPROCESS-OUTPUT Preprocessing Output Contract

      Stage 1 shall include prompt-schema normalization, ActiveBrief relation classification, retrieval-routing preparation, and PromptShaper / Job Structuring.

      Its output shall prepare the same SuperPrompt for both context paths and final composition.
    GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT
    4.11. PromptShaper Output Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-A2-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-1-PROMPT-FUNCTIONS Stage 1: Prompt Preprocessing and Prompt Functions
    STATEMENT: The PromptShaper internal function shall output the same SuperPrompt instance updated with structured job content and PromptShaper metadata.

    The output shall be suitable for retrieval-intent construction and both context paths.

    5. 5. Stage 2 - Knowledge Management Path
    Stage 2 is the document/project knowledge path. It produces selected and condensed document evidence for the current request.
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE
      3.3. Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path UID: GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-FOUR-STAGE-HIERARCHY Four Functional Runtime Stages
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: Stage 2 shall produce document/project knowledge context for the current request.

      It shall include document retrieval, document ranking/reranking behavior, document usefulness validation, and document evidence condensation.
    GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE
    5.1. Knowledge Management Runtime Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
    RELATIONS (Child):
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-INPUTS Knowledge Path Inputs
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-QUERY Document Retrieval Query Construction
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-DENSE Dense Document Retrieval Backend
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SPLADE SPLADE Document Retrieval Backend
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RRF Document Retrieval Fusion
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-HYDRATION Document Chunk Hydration
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SUPERPROMPT-WRITE Knowledge Context SuperPrompt Write-Back
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RERANKER Document ReRanker Boundary
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A3 Document Evidence Usefulness Validation
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A4 Document Evidence Condensation
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-OUTPUT Knowledge Path Output Contract
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-DOCUMENT-POSTPROCESS Document Post-Retrieval Coordination
    STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall produce document/project context from the active project stores.

    It shall include document retrieval, dense and sparse scoring, deterministic fusion, hydration, document reranking or pass-through, document usefulness validation, and document evidence condensation.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-INPUTS
      5.2. Knowledge Path Inputs UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-INPUTS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall use the current SuperPrompt, active project name, retrieval top-k value, document retrieval runtime settings, and retrieval intent/query text.

      It shall not use memory records as document chunks.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-QUERY
      5.3. Document Retrieval Query Construction UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-QUERY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The document retrieval query shall be built from the structured SuperPrompt body and routing metadata.

      For weak and irrelevant routing, the document retrieval query may be empty so that document retrieval is suppressed.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-DENSE
      5.4. Dense Document Retrieval Backend UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-DENSE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The document retrieval path shall support dense embedding retrieval over the active project Chroma document store.

      The dense backend shall receive neutral retrieval inputs and return ranked document rows to the higher-level Retriever.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SPLADE
      5.5. SPLADE Document Retrieval Backend UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SPLADE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The document retrieval path shall support SPLADE-based sparse retrieval over the active project SPLADE store.

      When a fixed dense candidate set is supplied, the SPLADE backend shall score those candidate IDs instead of independently replacing the candidate universe.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RRF
      5.6. Document Retrieval Fusion UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RRF STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The document retrieval path shall fuse dense and SPLADE ranked rows with deterministic weighted Reciprocal Rank Fusion.

      The fusion helper shall remain neutral and shall not own SuperPrompt, dense semantics, SPLADE semantics, or hydration logic.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-HYDRATION
      5.7. Document Chunk Hydration UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-HYDRATION STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The document Retriever shall hydrate selected ranked rows into real Chunk objects by reading the source files under the active doc_raw project root.

      Rows that point to stale or broken source files shall be skipped rather than crashing the entire document retrieval stage.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SUPERPROMPT-WRITE
      5.8. Knowledge Context SuperPrompt Write-Back UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SUPERPROMPT-WRITE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall write hydrated document chunks, retrieval views, A3-ready document candidate views, and selected document IDs into SuperPrompt.

      The document write-back fields shall remain separate from memory context fields.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RERANKER
      5.9. Document ReRanker Boundary UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RERANKER STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The document ReRanker shall operate only on document retrieval candidates already stored in SuperPrompt.

      When ColBERT reranking is disabled, the runtime shall keep the A3-ready document candidate view initialized by document retrieval.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A3
      5.10. Document Evidence Usefulness Validation UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A3 STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: A3 shall evaluate document candidates from the document candidate view.

      A3 shall not be responsible for memory record truth, memory candidate storage, or memory synthesis.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A4
      5.11. Document Evidence Condensation UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A4 STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: A4 shall condense selected document evidence after document retrieval, optional reranking, and A3 usefulness validation.

      A4 shall not replace the Memory Management compression and synthesis path.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-OUTPUT
      5.12. Knowledge Path Output Contract UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-OUTPUT STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall output document evidence artifacts in SuperPrompt.

      The output shall include document chunks and/or document evidence summary content suitable for final prompt composition.
    • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-DOCUMENT-POSTPROCESS
      8.5. Document Post-Retrieval Coordination UID: GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-DOCUMENT-POSTPROCESS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
      STATEMENT: After document retrieval, the runtime shall allow document reranking or pass-through, A3 usefulness validation, and A4 document evidence condensation.

      These operations belong to the Knowledge Management path.
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE
      5.1. Knowledge Management Runtime Scope UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
      RELATIONS (Child):
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-INPUTS Knowledge Path Inputs
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-QUERY Document Retrieval Query Construction
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-DENSE Dense Document Retrieval Backend
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SPLADE SPLADE Document Retrieval Backend
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RRF Document Retrieval Fusion
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-HYDRATION Document Chunk Hydration
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SUPERPROMPT-WRITE Knowledge Context SuperPrompt Write-Back
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-RERANKER Document ReRanker Boundary
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A3 Document Evidence Usefulness Validation
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-A4 Document Evidence Condensation
      • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-OUTPUT Knowledge Path Output Contract
      • GHOST-ORCH-COORDINATE-DOCUMENT-POSTPROCESS Document Post-Retrieval Coordination
      STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall produce document/project context from the active project stores.

      It shall include document retrieval, dense and sparse scoring, deterministic fusion, hydration, document reranking or pass-through, document usefulness validation, and document evidence condensation.
    GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-INPUTS
    5.2. Knowledge Path Inputs UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-INPUTS STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
    STATEMENT: The Knowledge Management path shall use the current SuperPrompt, active project name, retrieval top-k value, document retrieval runtime settings, and retrieval intent/query text.

    It shall not use memory records as document chunks.

      It shall include document retrieval, dense and sparse scoring, deterministic fusion, hydration, document reranking or pass-through, document usefulness validation, and document evidence condensation.
    GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-QUERY
    5.3. Document Retrieval Query Construction UID: GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-QUERY STATUS: Draft RELATIONS (Parent):
    • GHOST-ORCH-KNOWLEDGE-SCOPE Knowledge Management Runtime Scope
    STATEMENT: The document retrieval query shall be built from the structured SuperPrompt body and routing metadata.

    For weak and irrelevant routing, the document retrieval query may be empty so that document retrieval is suppressed.

    A4 shall not replace the Memory Management compression and synthesis path.

      • GHOST-ORCH-STAGE-2-KNOWLEDGE Stage 2: Knowledge Management Path
